Simulated Baseline: User access is provisioned as needed to support benefits case processing.
Control Deficiency: Lack of a formalized access provisioning workflow. There is no documented evidence of role justification or managerial approval prior to granting system access.
Remediation Strategy: Implement a formalized access request and approval workflow. Require and retain documentation of manager approval and role justification before provisioning access.

Simulated Baseline: Users frequently retain portal access after lateral movement, role changes, or shifting responsibilities.
Control Deficiency: Absence of a defined User Access Review (UAR) cadence, resulting in the accumulation of over-provisioned accounts and violations of the least privilege principle.
Remediation Strategy: Institute quarterly User Access Reviews (UAR) to validate that assigned roles remain appropriate. Enforce strict de-provisioning workflows for users undergoing role changes.

Simulated Baseline: Personnel report suspicious system activity on an ad-hoc basis when anomalies are noticed.
Control Deficiency: Lack of a formalized Incident Response Plan (IRP) detailing exact procedures for reporting, triaging, escalating, and containing security events.
Remediation Strategy: Develop and enforce a comprehensive Incident Response Plan (IRP) that establishes reporting workflows, severity classifications, escalation matrices, and containment timelines.

Simulated Baseline: The portal environment relies on various web applications, databases, and supporting cloud infrastructure.
Control Deficiency: Undefined vulnerability scanning cadence. There is no structured schedule to continuously monitor the infrastructure for emerging security weaknesses.
Remediation Strategy: Establish a recurring, automated vulnerability scanning cadence. Document scan frequencies, assign review owners, and implement a tracking mechanism to monitor findings through to closure.

Simulated Baseline: Security patches are applied ad-hoc when updates become available or IT identifies system degradation.
Control Deficiency: Absence of risk-based Service Level Agreements (SLAs) for patch management, increasing the exposure window for critical vulnerabilities.
Remediation Strategy: Define and enforce strict patch management SLAs based on risk severity (e.g., Critical vulnerabilities prioritized immediately, followed by High, Medium, and Low).

Simulated Baseline: The application generates basic activity logs capturing user authentication and case modifications.
Control Deficiency: Undefined audit logging and continuous monitoring policies. Expectations for log retention, review frequency, and designated reviewers are unassigned.
Remediation Strategy: Formalize audit logging requirements to capture authentications, failed access attempts, and administrative actions. Assign strict ownership for continuous log review and anomaly detection.

Simulated Baseline: Multiple cross-functional teams (case workers, IT support, security staff) engage during a security event.
Control Deficiency: Undefined Incident Response Team (IRT) roles and responsibilities, creating operational friction and communication delays during active security events.
Remediation Strategy: Clearly define IRT roles. Establish an escalation matrix dictating who investigates anomalies, who authorizes containment actions, and who approves stakeholder communications.

Simulated Baseline: Technical teams track open vulnerabilities internally following system reviews.
Control Deficiency: Lack of executive-level visibility. There is no standardized reporting mechanism to escalate overdue remediation items or recurring systemic weaknesses.
Remediation Strategy: Create a recurring vulnerability tracking dashboard for senior stakeholders detailing open findings, risk severity, assigned owners, and SLA compliance.

Simulated Baseline: The organization conducts informal discussions following the resolution of major security events.
Control Deficiency: Inconsistent post-incident Root Cause Analysis (RCA). The failure to document lessons learned results in missed process optimizations and recurring vulnerabilities.
Remediation Strategy: Standardize a post-incident Root Cause Analysis (RCA) template to formally document the incident timeline, containment efficacy, and strategic process improvements.
